title: 7 Herbal Essential Oils For Eye Strain
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great way to relieve eye strain. This is because they often contain compounds that help calm the eyes and reduce tension. For example, lavender oil (Lavandula angustifolia) has a soothing effect, which can help relax the muscles around the eyes. Lemon balm oil (Melissa officinalis) has a calming effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can lead to eye strain. Rosemary oil (Rosmarinus officinalis) can also help improve focus and concentration, which can be beneficial when working on a computer or reading for long periods.  Herbal teas can also relieve eye strain because they allow you to relax and take a break from screens. Drinking a cup of herbal tea can help calm your mind and body, reducing tension and stress that can contribute to eye strain. When you drink herbal tea, you're not just hydrating your body, you're also giving your eyes a break from the constant strain of focusing on a screen.  Using herbal essential oils and drinking herbal teas can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help you reduce eye strain, which can lead to headaches and fatigue. By relieving eye strain, you can also improve your sleep quality, as you're not waking up in the middle of the night with eye pain or headaches. Additionally, using herbal essential oils and drinking herbal teas can help you relax and reduce stress, which can improve your overall well-being and mood.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for eye strain

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents that contribute to its therapeutic properties. These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce eye strain caused by prolonged screen time or reading. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its high concentration of linalool, can also help alleviate eye fatigue and discomfort. Additionally, the oil's ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress can help reduce eye strain caused by tension and fatigue. By applying Lavandula angustifolia essential oils topically or inhaling its vapors, individuals may experience relief from eye strain and promote overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 2 cups of water, and a clean glass jar.
Place the flowers in the jar and pour in the water. Let it sit for 2-3 hours to allow the flowers to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat (around 100°F to 150°F).
Continue heating the liquid until the water has evaporated, leaving behind a concentrated essential oil. Let it cool before storing it in a dark glass bottle for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that can help soothe irritated eyes. The essential oil's antispasmodic properties can also help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension that may contribute to eye strain. Additionally, melissa officinalis essential oils has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by prolonged screen time. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, melissa officinalis essential oils may be a useful natural remedy for alleviating eye strain.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), a glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a bowl.
Wash and dry the Melissa officinalis leaves. Use 1 tablespoon of leaves per 1 cup of carrier oil.
Combine the dried leaves and carrier oil in the glass jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture using the cheesecloth and bowl. Discard the solids and keep the liquid.
Bottle the liquid in a dark glass bottle and use it for eye strain relief. Apply 2-3 drops to your eyes, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye strain caused by prolonged computer use or reading. Camphor and borneol have a cooling effect on the skin, which can be beneficial in reducing puffiness and redness associated with eye strain. The antioxidant properties of bornyl acetate help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds can also help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes and surrounding areas.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it reaches 100°F to 110°F (38°C to 43°C).
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the water and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Use a dropper to transfer the essential oil into small bottles. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovaleranone as its biologically active constituents. These compounds have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye strain caused by prolonged computer use or reading. The sedative properties of valerenic acid and valeranone promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, which can contribute to eye strain. The anti-inflammatory properties of isovaleranone and valerenic acid may help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, further alleviating eye strain. When inhaled, the essential oil's calming properties can also help decrease stress and promote a sense of calm, which can further benefit individuals experiencing eye strain.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis roots, 2 cups of water, and a saucepan.
Place the roots in the saucepan and add the water. Heat the mixture on low heat for 30 minutes.
Let the mixture cool down, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a still or a steam distillation apparatus. Distill the liquid to separate the essential oil from the water.
Collect the essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle. Use it for eye strain relief by applying a few drops to a cloth and placing it over your eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds help to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation, making it easier to alleviate eye strain. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that protect the eyes from damage and oxidative stress. The terpenoids, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, help to relax the eye muscles and reduce tension, providing relief from eye strain. By combining these properties, Ginkgo biloba essential oils can help to reduce eye fatigue and promote overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Use a coffee grinder or herb grinder to finely chop the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Combine the chopped leaves with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains a high concentration of limonene, geranial, and neral, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce eye strain caused by prolonged computer use or reading by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress on the eyes. The essential oils' ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation can also help alleviate eye strain by calming the eye muscles and reducing eye fatigue. Additionally, the citrus scent of Cymbopogon citratus essential oils has been shown to improve mood and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to eye strain. By using Cymbopogon citratus essential oils, you may experience relief from eye strain and improve your overall eye health.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat, then add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves and remove from heat.
Let the mixture steep for 2 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the mixture to a still or a distillation apparatus to separate the oil from the water.
Collect the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it for eye strain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains compounds like anethole, limonene, and fenchone, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by eye strain. The antioxidant activity of limonene and fenchone also helps to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain. Foeniculum vulgare essential oils has a relaxing effect on the eyes and can help to reduce fatigue and tension. By reducing inflammation and protecting the eyes from damage, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to alleviate eye strain.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for eye strain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or sweet almond oil).
Heat 2 cups of carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over boiling water.
Add 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the heated carrier oil and stir well.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight, then strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Discard the seeds and store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent eye strain. Some herbal teas, like those containing chamomile or peppermint, can calm the eyes and reduce tension. The antioxidants in herbal teas can also protect the eyes from damage. This might help you feel more comfortable when using digital devices or reading for long periods.
faq_safe: You can consume herbal teas for eye strain daily, but in moderation. Overdoing it can lead to caffeine overload, interacting with medications, or causing stomach problems. Choose teas like peppermint, chamomile, or passionflower that are known for their calming effects on the eyes and body. Just be mindful of your body's reaction.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with eye strain, but it's hard to say exactly how long it takes to notice results. Some people feel relief after 30 minutes to an hour, while others might need to drink a cup or two before they start to feel better.
faq_time: For eye strain, a warm cup of herbal tea in the evening can be soothing. It helps relax the eyes and the mind after a long day. As you unwind before bed, herbal tea can calm your senses and ease fatigue, making it a great way to end your day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, and this can be concerning if you're taking them for eye strain. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and valerian root, can slow down your body's processing of medications, making them less effective. This is because these teas contain compounds that can affect the way your body works.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and experiencing eye strain, herbal teas might seem like a soothing solution. However, it's best to be cautious. Some herbs, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe, but others, like pennyroyal and sage, might not be. Always check the ingredients and amounts to ensure they're not harming you or your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for eye strain while breastfeeding are generally considered safe. Look for teas with caffeine-free herbs like chamomile or peppermint. These teas can help relax your eyes and body. Some herbs, like chamomile, are even used to calm babies, so they're a good choice for breastfeeding mothers.
